# Nature Biotechnology submission record

This file records the public, non-secret submission metadata for the initial Nature Biotechnology submission.

## Submission

- Journal: Nature Biotechnology
- Manuscript tracking number: NBT-BC72046
- MTS manuscript ID: 83994
- Revision: 0
- Submitted: 19 May 2026
- Article type in cover letter: Brief Communication
- Manuscript title: Foundation-model morphology maps residual programs in spatial-omics contours
- Local submitted package: `docs/_static/tutorials/multimodal_histoseg_lazyslide_breast_wta/naturebiotech_package/NBT_UPLOAD_ONLY_20260517`
- GitHub repository: `https://github.com/hutaobo/pyXenium`
- GitHub release cited in submission system: `https://github.com/hutaobo/pyXenium/releases/tag/nbt-submission-upload-20260518-draft`
- Repository commit at post-submission record creation: `0b58ce1`

Do not commit the full MTS view URL because it contains `ms_id_key`, which should be treated as a private access token. Use the MTS dashboard or the tracking number above for correspondence.

## MTS policy selections

- Show author information: do not show author information to reviewers.
- Research Square author dashboard: acknowledged.
- Preprint deposition: no In Review deposition requested.
- Competing interests: no competing interest.
- Applicable funding source: no applicable funding.
- Previous interactions: content related to the submission was recently published by the journal.
- Research data deposition: no Figshare deposition requested.
- Code availability: yes, central code was developed.
- Code Ocean support: no, because code is already available through the public GitHub repository/release.

## Next actions

1. Keep the submitted package unchanged unless the editorial office requests a correction.
2. Monitor MTS status and email for technical checks, editor assignment or editorial decision.
3. Continue any exploratory A100 analyses in separate run directories only; do not modify the submitted manuscript or upload package until a journal request or revision decision arrives.
4. If the editorial office requests a replacement file, prepare a small dated replacement package and update this record.
